raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Asian Americans are reporting a dramatic increase in harassment, paralleling the rise in anti-Asian hate incidents offline.",
    "LGBTQ+ individuals are experiencing harassment at the highest levels among all respondents.",
    "Nearly half of youth ages 13-17 report experiencing some type of harassment, and more than a third in the past 12 months.",
    "New studies draw ties between hate-motivated violence, mass shootings, and online activity.",
    "Assailants in several mass shootings used social media to engage in hateful activity and were often radicalized online.",
    "A recent bulletin by the Department of Homeland Security warned of extremist, copycat behavior in online forums following the Uvalde shooting.",
    "Social media platforms have been widely criticized for grossly inadequate efforts to self-police against hate and disinformation.",
    "Social media platforms will recommend harmful, divisive, or false content even when users are not actively seeking it.",
    "Facebook's algorithms encourage polarization and exploit the human brain's attraction to divisiveness.",
    "AB 587 would require social media platforms to disclose their corporate policies on hate speech, disinformation, extremism, harassment, and foreign political interference.",
    "AB 587 mandates platforms disclose key metrics regarding flagged content, including the number of pieces of content and the type of action taken.",
    "According to ADL’s Online Hate and Harassment Report, 65% of those in a marginalized group experienced hate-based harassment online because of their identity.",
    "Greater transparency can combat the rise of disinformation, hate speech, and calls to violence on social media.",
    "AB 587 seeks to increase public trust in social media platforms by requiring disclosure of their policies and enforcement practices."
  ]
}
```
